free_text: Measurements of prompt fission neutron spectra and
Fission neutron spectra for 238U and 232Th given
Provided a pulsed proton or deuteron beam with bursts
of 1.5 - 2 ns duration at a repetition rate of 1 or
2 MHz, and an average beam current of 4 - 6 uA.
En=1.2 and 2.0 MeV (energy spread 90 keV at 0 deg)
En=4.2 and 6.1 MeV (energy spread 300 keV at 0 deg)
En=14.1 MeV (energy spread 300 keV at 97.5 deg)
Flight path of ~5 m. Time resolution was 1.5 ns.
The detector was equipped with pulse shape
Monitored spectrum and intensity of source neutrons.
Cylinders of elemental uranium or thorium.
2 cm in diameter and 5 cm long, encased in Al cans.
The samples were placed 10 or 12 cm from the neutron-
- finite energy resolution of tof spectrometer.
